title: Roasted Peach Crostini with Lemon Ricotta & Basil
description: Toasted baguette topped with lemony ricotta and caramelized roasted peaches, finished with fresh basil. Elegant, seasonal, and mostly make-ahead.

ingredients.name: French baguettes, cut into 1/2-inch diagonal slices (~22 slices)
fresh peaches, sliced into 1/2-inch wedges (~3 medium peaches)
honey or maple syrup (optional, helps caramelization)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment. Wash peaches, halve along the natural seam, remove pits, and slice into 1/2-inch wedges.
Toss peach slices with olive oil and honey (if using). Spread in a single layer on the baking sheet. Roast 20–25 minutes until softened and caramelized at the edges.
Make the ricotta spread: stir together ricotta, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper until smooth. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Toast baguette slices: grill 2 minutes per side, or bake at 400°F for 5–7 minutes until golden.
Spread about 1 tbsp lemon ricotta on each toasted slice. Top with 1–2 roasted peach slices. Finish with basil ribbons. Serve immediately.

notes:
Toast and assemble right before serving -- bread gets soggy quickly once topped.

Make-ahead: roast peaches up to 1 day ahead (store covered in fridge, rewarm at 300°F for 5–7 minutes). Ricotta mixture keeps refrigerated for 1–2 days -- stir before using.

Ripe peaches matter here. If peaches are underripe, increase honey and add 5 minutes to the roast time.
